steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "When you place your ______ the pitch will shift as your hands move through the air." The blank fills a noun phrase meaning 'hands between the two antennas' followed by a comma. The key grammar question is whether any apostrophes are needed — apostrophes mark possession or contractions, never simple plurals.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — hand's between the two antenna's,: 'hand's' implies belonging to a hand; 'antenna's' implies belonging to an antenna. Neither word is possessive here. ✗
  • Choice B — hands between the two antennas,: plain plurals with no apostrophes — you place multiple hands between multiple antennas. No possession, no contraction. ✓
  • Choice C — hands' between the two antennas',: 'hands'' and 'antennas'' both carry apostrophes marking possession, but neither word possesses anything in context. ✗
  • Choice D — hands' between the two antennas,: 'hands'' still incorrectly marks a plain plural as possessive. ✗
That is the correct answer.
